title: UNICEF Philippines Social and Behavior Change (SBC) Officer Opportunity 2026 – Full-Time National Volunteer Role in Manila
summary: If you are passionate about creating meaningful social impact, influencing positive behavioral change, and contributing to children’s welfare, this opportunity with UNICEF Philippines could be your next career breakthrough. The Social and Behavior Change (SBC) Officer role offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of communication, behavioral science, and development programming while supporting national priorities and global Sustainable Development Goals. Overview of the Opportunity The Social and Behavior Change (SBC) Officer position is a full-time, onsite national UN Volunteer assignment based in Manila, Philippines. This 12-month role (with the possibility of extension) is designed for professionals eager to support UNICEF’s mission of promoting and protecting children’s rights, especially for the most vulnerable populations. Location: Manila, Philippines Start Date: 25 May 2026 Duration: 12 months (extendable) Work Schedule: Full-time Volunteer Type: National Specialist (UN Volunteer) Work Mode: Onsite (UN premises) Application Deadline: 12 May 2026 About UNICEF’s Mission and SBC Approach UNICEF works globally to ensure every child thrives, focusing on health, education, protection, and equality. A key strategy in achieving these outcomes is Social and Behavior Change (SBC), which aims to: Strengthen community engagement Improve service uptake Influence positive behaviors and social norms Address barriers related to knowledge, attitudes, and practices The SBC Officer will play a central role in advancing these objectives by supporting evidence-based strategies and fostering collaboration across sectors. Key Responsibilities and Duties The role is structured across five major functional areas: 1. Capacity Development for SBC Facilitate learning programs and training sessions for UNICEF staff and partners Conduct training needs assessments and evaluate learning outcomes Maintain detailed documentation and reporting of capacity-building activities 2. Technical Support to Programme Sectors Provide strategic and operational SBC support across sectors such as health, education, and child protection Assist in designing, implementing, and monitoring SBC interventions Conduct quality assurance reviews of program materials and plans 3. SBC Systems Strengthening and Integration Support policy dialogues and multi-stakeholder engagement Contribute to integrating SBC into academic and professional learning systems Prepare concept notes, reports, and meeting summaries 4. Knowledge Management and Network Engagement Develop knowledge products such as case studies, reports, and presentations Support participation in conferences and workshops Manage and organize SBC knowledge repositories 5. Monitoring, Reporting, and Programme Learning Track program results and compile reports Analyze data to improve program effectiveness Document lessons learned for continuous improvement Volunteerism and Additional Contributions As a UN Volunteer, you are also expected to: Promote volunteerism and community engagement Participate in UNV events such as International Volunteer Day Contribute articles and reports on field experiences Support onboarding of new UN Volunteers Encourage the use of online volunteering platforms Required Qualifications and Experience Education Bachelor’s degree in fields such as: Development Communication Behavioral Science Sociology, Psychology, Anthropology Communication for Development or related disciplines Advanced degrees are an added advantage Experience Minimum of 3 years in: Social and Behavior Change Communication for Development Program management or related fields Experience in emergency or humanitarian settings is preferred Languages Fluent in English and Filipino Core Competencies Successful candidates will demonstrate: Strong technical knowledge of SBC strategies High ethical standards and integrity Excellent teamwork and respect for diversity Commitment to continuous learning Strong planning, communication, and analytical skills Flexibility and adaptability in dynamic environments Benefits and Entitlements As a National UN Volunteer, you will receive: Monthly Volunteer Living Allowance (VLA) Entry and Exit Lump Sum Allowances Comprehensive insurance coverage Annual leave (2.5 days per month) Access to UN learning and career development resources Living Conditions Volunteers are responsible for arranging their own accommodation and daily living needs. Manila offers access to essential services including healthcare, markets, and communication infrastructure. Important Notes UNICEF is an equal opportunity organization committed to diversity and inclusion Reasonable accommodations are available for persons with disabilities COVID-19 vaccination is required for onsite roles No fees are charged at any stage of the recruitment process How to Apply Interested candidates should apply through the official UN Volunteer platform before the deadline. Ensure your application highlights relevant experience, technical skills, and alignment with UNICEF’s mission. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Volunteering Opportunities on OFY here . title: USDA LAMP Grants 2026: Complete Guide to Local Agriculture Market Program Funding, Eligibility, Deadlines & How to Apply
summary: The U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA), through its Agricultural Marketing Service (AMS), offers one of the most comprehensive funding opportunities for farmers, agribusinesses, and food system innovators: the Local Agriculture Market Program (LAMP) . If you are looking to expand your agricultural business, strengthen local food systems, or access funding for value-added production, this in-depth guide will walk you through everything you need to know about LAMP—its structure, funding streams, benefits, and how to apply successfully. What is the Local Agriculture Market Program (LAMP)? The Local Agriculture Market Program (LAMP) is a federally funded initiative established under the 2018 Farm Bill to support the development and expansion of: Direct-to-consumer agricultural marketing Local and regional food markets Food enterprises and distribution systems Value-added agricultural products LAMP acts as an umbrella program , consolidating multiple grant opportunities designed to build resilient, efficient, and profitable food systems across the United States. Primary Goals of the LAMP Program The program is structured to deliver measurable impact across the agricultural ecosystem through the following objectives: 1. Strengthening Regional Food Economies Promote collaboration between public and private sector stakeholders Encourage partnerships that enhance food system resilience 2. Supporting Business Development Fund feasibility studies and strategic planning Enable producers to build scalable and sustainable operations 3. Expanding Value-Added Agriculture Support innovation in food processing, packaging, and branding Increase profitability through product diversification 4. Enhancing Food System Infrastructure Improve storage, aggregation, and distribution networks Strengthen supply chain efficiency 5. Increasing Economic Opportunities Boost income for farmers and food businesses Create employment opportunities in local communities 6. Simplifying Grant Processes Streamline application procedures Improve reporting and compliance systems LAMP Funding Opportunities (Grant Programs Explained) LAMP funding is distributed across four major grant programs. Each serves a distinct purpose within the agricultural value chain: 1. Farmers Market Promotion Program (FMPP) Focus: Direct-to-consumer marketing Supports: Farmers markets Roadside stands Community-supported agriculture (CSA) Apply Here Deadlines: Deadlines vary annually. Applicants should check the official page for current funding cycles. 2. Local Food Promotion Program (LFPP) Focus: Local and regional food business development Supports: Food hubs Aggregation and distribution systems Value chain coordination Apply Here Deadlines: Deadlines vary depending on funding announcements. 3. Regional Food System Partnerships Program (RFSP) Focus: Multi-stakeholder regional collaboration Supports: Partnerships between producers, businesses, and institutions Regional food system planning and coordination Apply Here Deadlines: Application timelines differ by funding cycle. 4. Value-Added Producer Grants (VAPG) Administered by: USDA Rural Development Focus: Increasing product value and marketability Supports: Processing and packaging Branding and marketing strategies Product innovation Apply Here Deadlines: Deadlines vary and are announced per funding round. Who Should Apply for LAMP Grants? LAMP is designed for a broad range of stakeholders within the agricultural and food systems sector: Farmers and agricultural producers Food entrepreneurs and agribusiness owners Cooperatives and producer groups Nonprofit organizations Food system networks and alliances Public-private partnerships Key Benefits of the LAMP Program Participating in LAMP provides both financial and strategic advantages: 1. Access to Federal Funding Grants to support business growth and innovation Funding for infrastructure and operational improvements 2. Market Expansion Opportunities Improved access to local and regional markets Enhanced consumer reach 3. Strengthened Capacity Better operational efficiency Improved supply chain systems 4. Community and Economic Impact Job creation Increased local economic activity 5. Long-Term Sustainability Support for environmentally and economically sustainable practices Additional Resources for Applicants To help applicants succeed, USDA provides several support tools: LAMP Grant Toolkits Application Support LAMP Infographic (PDF) LAMP Report to Congress Seeds of Success Series (case studies and success stories) LAMP Navigator Tool (explore funded projects and impact) FY25 LAMP Webinar Recording These resources provide guidance on application preparation, project design, and implementation. How to Apply for LAMP Grants Follow these steps to submit a strong application: Step 1: Identify the Right Program Choose the grant that aligns with your business or project goals. Step 2: Review Guidelines Carefully read eligibility criteria and application instructions. Step 3: Prepare Your Proposal Include: Business plan or project outline Budget and financial projections Expected outcomes and impact Step 4: Use Available Resources Leverage USDA toolkits and webinars to strengthen your application. Step 5: Submit Your Application Apply through the official USDA grant portals linked above. Important Notes on Deadlines There is no single universal deadline for the LAMP program. Each grant program (FMPP, LFPP, RFSP, VAPG) has its own application timeline. Applicants are advised to: Regularly check the official USDA website Monitor funding announcements Apply early within open cycles Why LAMP Matters The Local Agriculture Market Program plays a critical role in transforming the agricultural landscape by: Strengthening food security Supporting small and mid-sized producers Encouraging innovation in agriculture Building resilient and sustainable food systems It is a key driver of economic growth and sustainability within the U.S. agricultural sector. Final Thoughts The USDA LAMP Grants Program is more than just funding—it is a strategic investment in the future of agriculture. By supporting innovation, collaboration, and market expansion, LAMP empowers producers and organizations to thrive in an evolving global food system. If you are ready to scale your agricultural impact, now is the time to explore and apply for LAMP funding opportunities. For more information about this grant visit HERE Discover more grant opportunities from OFY Here The post USDA LAMP Grants 2026: Complete Guide to Local Agriculture Market Program Funding, Eligibility, Deadlines How to Apply appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: OpenAI Safety Fellowship 2026: A Transformative Opportunity for AI Researchers to Shape the Future of Safe and Ethical AI
summary: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence has brought unprecedented opportunities—and equally significant challenges. As AI systems grow more powerful, the importance of ensuring their safety, reliability, and ethical alignment becomes critical. Recognizing this, OpenAI has launched an exciting new initiative: the OpenAI Safety Fellowship . This pilot fellowship program is designed to support independent researchers, engineers, and practitioners in conducting high-impact work focused on AI safety and alignment. If you are passionate about shaping the future of responsible AI, this opportunity could be a pivotal step in your career. Overview of the OpenAI Safety Fellowship The OpenAI Safety Fellowship is a structured program aimed at nurturing the next generation of experts in AI safety. It combines financial support, mentorship, and access to cutting-edge resources to empower fellows to produce meaningful research. Key Program Details Program Duration: September 14, 2026 – February 5, 2027 Application Deadline: May 3, 2026 Notification of Results: July 25, 2026 Work Mode: In-person workspace available in Berkeley Remote participation is also supported Fellowship Objectives The program focuses on advancing research that addresses critical safety challenges in modern and future AI systems. OpenAI is particularly interested in work that is: Empirically grounded Technically rigorous Relevant to real-world AI systems Valuable to the broader research community Priority Research Areas Applicants are encouraged to propose projects in key domains of AI safety and alignment. These include, but are not limited to: Safety Evaluation Developing benchmarks and methods to assess AI system safety Ethics in AI Addressing fairness, accountability, and societal impacts Robustness Ensuring AI systems perform reliably under diverse conditions Scalable Mitigations Designing solutions that scale with increasingly powerful AI systems Privacy-Preserving Safety Methods Protecting user data while maintaining safety standards Agentic Oversight Monitoring and controlling autonomous AI agents High-Severity Misuse Prevention Reducing risks from malicious or unintended misuse of AI What Fellows Will Receive The OpenAI Safety Fellowship offers a comprehensive support system to ensure fellows can focus on impactful research. Benefits Include: Monthly Stipend Financial support to cover living expenses Compute Resources Access to computational tools necessary for advanced AI research API Credits Resources to experiment and build using OpenAI technologies Mentorship Direct guidance from experienced researchers and professionals Collaborative Environment Engagement with a cohort of fellows and access to a shared workspace Fellowship Expectations Participants in the program are expected to: Conduct rigorous and independent research Collaborate with mentors and peers Produce a substantial research output , such as: A research paper A benchmark A dataset This output should contribute meaningfully to the field of AI safety and alignment. Who Should Apply? The fellowship welcomes individuals from a wide range of disciplines, emphasizing capability over credentials. Relevant Backgrounds Include: Computer Science Social Sciences Cybersecurity Privacy Research Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) Related interdisciplinary fields Selection Criteria: Strong research ability Sound technical judgment Proven execution skills Commitment to AI safety Applicants must also provide reference contacts as part of the application. Application Process Interested candidates can Click here to know more and to apply Important Dates Recap: Applications Open: Already open Deadline: May 3, 2026 Selection Notification: July 25, 2026 Why This Fellowship Matters The OpenAI Safety Fellowship is more than just a research program—it is a strategic initiative to build a global community of experts dedicated to ensuring AI systems remain safe, ethical, and aligned with human values. By participating, fellows gain: Exposure to real-world AI challenges Opportunities to influence the future of AI policy and development A platform to publish impactful work Connections with leading experts in the field For more global fellowship opportunities Click here The post OpenAI Safety Fellowship 2026: A Transformative Opportunity for AI Researchers to Shape the Future of Safe and Ethical AI appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Ford Philanthropy Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded): Global Opportunity for Entrepreneurs and Community Leaders Driving Impact and Resilience
summary: The Ford Philanthropy Fellowship 2026 , powered by the Watson Institute , is a prestigious, fully funded global fellowship designed to empower entrepreneurs and community leaders who are building impactful solutions in areas such as community development, education, and disaster preparedness . This highly competitive program equips selected fellows with entrepreneurial training, mentorship, financial tools, and a global network , enabling them to scale their ventures and strengthen communities worldwide. As detailed in the official program overview , the fellowship is tailored for leaders who are already making measurable impact and are ready to expand their reach and sustainability. About the Ford Philanthropy Fellowship The Ford Philanthropy Fellowship is an immersive leadership and entrepreneurship program designed to: Support local changemakers addressing real-world challenges Build resilient and thriving communities Strengthen ventures focused on education, workforce development, and disaster response Foster innovation and sustainable community impact The fellowship combines structured learning, mentorship, and hands-on implementation to create a transformative experience for participants. What the Fellowship Offers The program delivers a comprehensive set of benefits aimed at accelerating entrepreneurial growth and leadership development. 1. Business Leadership Workshops Fellows participate in hands-on workshops covering: Entrepreneurship and business development Financial literacy and management Strategic leadership and decision-making Scaling impact-driven ventures These sessions are led by experienced entrepreneurs and global experts. 2. Financial Tools and Resources Participants gain access to: Practical financial frameworks Funding strategies and insights Tools to strengthen venture sustainability Guidance from financial experts and practitioners This ensures fellows are equipped to manage and grow their ventures effectively. 3. Basecamps: Community Impact Initiatives A unique feature of the fellowship is the Basecamp experience , where fellows: Design and lead a 1-day community workshop Support early-stage entrepreneurs Strengthen local entrepreneurial ecosystems Receive training and a stipend to implement the initiative This ensures direct, localized impact within each fellow’s community. 4. Pitch Training and Virtual Summit Fellows receive dedicated training to: Develop compelling venture pitches Refine storytelling and communication Present their work to stakeholders and investors The program culminates in a Virtual Summit (January 21, 2027) where fellows showcase their ventures. 5. Mentorship and Global Network Participants benefit from: One-on-one mentorship Access to a global network of entrepreneurs and leaders Long-term alumni support This network continues to provide opportunities even after the fellowship ends. Experts and Facilitators Fellows learn directly from industry leaders and practitioners. Previous experts include: Cole Hoover – Strategic Facilitator and Purpose Coach Kimberly Moore – EdTech CEO and Education Strategist Sarah Windrum – Business Leader and Innovation Expert These experts provide both group training and personalized guidance. Program Structure and Duration The fellowship runs from: Start Date: September 8, 2026 End Date: January 21, 2027 Key Components: Virtual immersive experience Weekly workshops (Tuesday–Thursday) Mentorship sessions Community-based Basecamp project Final Summit presentation Weekly Commitment: Approximately 8–10 hours per week Important Dates Applicants should take note of the following timeline: Priority Application Deadline: August 15, 2026 Program Start: September 8, 2026 Virtual Summit: January 21, 2027 Final Selection Announcement: August 26, 2026 Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early application is strongly encouraged. Eligibility Criteria The fellowship is designed for individuals who are already making measurable impact in their communities. Who Can Apply: Entrepreneurs or Community Leaders Leading initiatives that support community development Addressing education, resilience, or disaster-related challenges Experience and Impact Ventures operating for 1–5 years Fewer than 10 employees Demonstrated measurable impact Funding and Traction Secured funding (grants, donations, investments), or Generating revenue Commitment Must fully participate in all program activities Age Requirement Must be 18 years or older Eligible Countries Applicants are encouraged to apply if they are creating impact in: Germany Mexico South Africa Thailand Brazil Vietnam Priority is given to those working in Ford plant communities and regions affected by recurring disasters . Check the full eligibility requirements HERE . Fellowship Impact and Track Record Since its inception, the program has achieved remarkable results: Supported 99 Fellows Impacted over 4,600 entrepreneurs Created 2,435 jobs Raised over $18.6 million in capital This demonstrates the fellowship’s strong commitment to real-world impact and community transformation. Why You Should Apply The Ford Philanthropy Fellowship stands out because it: Is fully funded Focuses on real-world community impact Provides hands-on entrepreneurial training Offers global mentorship and networking Supports long-term leadership development It is not just a fellowship—it is a platform for building sustainable change. Application Process The selection process is structured to identify high-potential leaders: Online Application Takes approximately 30 minutes Submit before the priority deadline Application Review Evaluation by the admissions committee Interview Stage Selected candidates attend a 20-minute virtual interview Final Selection Results announced by August 26, 2026 Click HERE to start your application! For more information about the Ford Philanthropy Fellowship 2026, click HERE . Conclusion The Ford Philanthropy Fellowship 2026 is a powerful opportunity for entrepreneurs and community leaders to elevate their work, expand their impact, and connect with a global network of changemakers. If you are committed to strengthening communities and building resilient systems, this fellowship provides the tools, mentorship, and support needed to scale your vision. Discover more global Fellowship , Leadership Development and Entrepreneurship opportunities on OFY HERE. title: Program Coordinator/Research Assistant Role at CSIS Americas Program: A Gateway to Policy, Research, and Global Impact
summary: The Center for Strategic and International Studies (CSIS), one of the world’s leading bipartisan policy research organizations, has announced an exciting full-time opportunity within its renowned Americas Program. The Program is currently accepting applications for the role of Program Coordinator/Research Assistant (PC/RA) —a dynamic position designed for professionals eager to contribute to impactful policy research and regional engagement across Latin America and the Caribbean. This opportunity is ideal for individuals who are highly organized, detail-oriented, and proactive, with a strong passion for international affairs and public policy. The selected candidate will play a crucial role in supporting research initiatives, coordinating high-level events, and ensuring the smooth functioning of program operations. About the Role The Program Coordinator/Research Assistant position is a hybrid role that combines research, administrative support, and program coordination. It offers hands-on exposure to policy development and international relations, particularly within the context of the Americas. The role is based in Washington, D.C. , placing the candidate at the heart of global policy discourse and institutional collaboration. Key Responsibilities The successful candidate will contribute across multiple functional areas, including: 1. Research and Writing Support Assist in conducting research on topics related to Latin America and the Caribbean Contribute to reports, briefs, and policy papers Support ongoing research projects and data collection 2. Event Coordination Organize and manage both public and private events Coordinate logistics such as scheduling, invitations, and venue arrangements Assist in stakeholder engagement and follow-ups 3. Communications and Outreach Support the development of communication materials Assist in outreach efforts to expand program visibility Collaborate with internal teams to maintain consistent messaging 4. Administrative and Operational Support Provide day-to-day administrative assistance Ensure smooth workflow and operational efficiency Manage documentation, scheduling, and coordination tasks Location and Work Environment Location: Washington, D.C. Work within a fast-paced, collaborative policy environment Engage with experts, policymakers, and researchers Language Requirements Spanish fluency is required Portuguese proficiency is considered an advantage These language skills are essential given the program’s regional focus on Latin America and the Caribbean. Who Should Apply This role is best suited for candidates who: Thrive in a fast-paced and intellectually stimulating environment Possess strong organizational and multitasking abilities Have excellent research and writing skills Are proactive and capable of working both independently and collaboratively Why This Opportunity Stands Out Gain hands-on experience in a globally recognized think tank Work on real-world policy issues impacting the Americas Build a strong professional network in international affairs Develop expertise in research, communications, and program management Contribute to meaningful discussions shaping regional and global policy Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by May 15, 2026 . How to Apply Interested candidates can apply through the official application portal HERE . Click HERE for more information about the Program Coordinator/Research Assistant Role at CSIS Americas Program. Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . title: Western Union Global Fellowship 2026 (Fully Funded): Empowering Entrepreneurs Supporting Refugee and Marginalized Communities Worldwide
summary: The Western Union Global Fellowship 2026 , powered by the Watson Institute , is a highly competitive, fully funded virtual fellowship designed to support entrepreneurs and leaders working with highly marginalized, refugee, and displaced communities across the globe. This transformative fellowship equips participants with the skills, mentorship, funding strategies, and global networks needed to scale their ventures and create sustainable economic opportunities in underserved regions. As highlighted in the official toolkit , this program is tailored for impact-driven leaders who are already building solutions and are ready to accelerate their growth and expand their reach. About the Western Union Global Fellowship The Western Union Global Fellowship is a 16-week intensive virtual program focused on strengthening ventures that address economic inequality and social challenges affecting displaced and underserved populations. The fellowship aims to: Equip entrepreneurs with advanced business and leadership skills Strengthen ventures working in fragile and underserved contexts Foster global collaboration among changemakers Drive economic resilience in vulnerable communities Participants gain access to a structured learning environment combined with hands-on support to scale their impact effectively. Key Benefits of the Fellowship The program offers a comprehensive suite of benefits designed to accelerate entrepreneurial growth and impact. 1. Expert-Led Entrepreneurial Training Fellows receive training in critical areas such as: Product-market fit Data analysis and performance metrics Funding and investment strategies Team management and leadership Pitch development These sessions are delivered by experienced entrepreneurs and industry experts. 2. World-Class Mentorship Participants benefit from: Mentorship from globally recognized entrepreneurs and leaders Guidance from fellowship alumni Personalized feedback on business strategies This mentorship ensures fellows receive practical insights tailored to their ventures. 3. Pitch Coaching and Venture Scaling The fellowship includes dedicated pitch training to help participants: Refine their storytelling Improve investor readiness Communicate impact effectively This culminates in a final pitch presentation at the program’s Summit. 4. Basecamp Community Impact Initiative ($1,000 Stipend) A standout feature of the fellowship is the Basecamp initiative , where fellows: Organize a local community event Engage and support early-stage entrepreneurs Receive a $1,000 stipend to execute their initiative This ensures fellows create direct impact within their communities. 5. Access to a Global Network Participants join a powerful ecosystem of: Entrepreneurs Investors Mentors Global partners This network continues to support fellows beyond the program. Fellowship Duration and Structure The Western Union Global Fellowship runs for approximately 16 weeks (4 months) and is fully virtual, making it accessible to participants across different countries. The program includes: Weekly workshops Mentorship sessions Peer collaboration Venture development activities Final Summit presentation Important Dates Applicants should carefully note the following timeline: Application Opens: April 6, 2026 Priority Deadline: June 28, 2026 Program Start Date: August 11, 2026 Final Summit: December 3, 2026 The Summit serves as a culminating event where fellows present their ventures and showcase their progress. Eligibility Criteria The fellowship targets entrepreneurs who are already building impactful solutions. Applicants Must: Operate a Venture Either nonprofit or for-profit Stage of Venture At the problem-solution or product-market fit stage Must demonstrate measurable impact Traction Requirements Generating revenue, or Secured funding (grants, investments, donations), or Built a strong user base Target Beneficiaries Must serve highly marginalized, refugee, or displaced communities Commitment Must fully participate in the entire 4-month program Check full eligibility requirements HERE ! Eligible Countries Applicants must be creating impact in one or more of the following countries: United States Costa Rica India Philippines Lithuania Argentina Italy Brazil Peru Fellowship Cohort Size The program is highly selective, with approximately: 20 Fellows selected globally This ensures a personalized and high-impact learning experience. How to Apply Applicants can apply directly through the official fellowship platform: Application Portal: https://watson.is/western-union-global-fellowship-application/ Program Page: https://watson.is/western-union-global-fellowship Nomination Opportunity In addition to direct applications, individuals can nominate entrepreneurs. A nomination link is available for referring candidates Selected nominees will be contacted by the fellowship team This expands access to deserving leaders who may not apply independently. Why You Should Apply The Western Union Global Fellowship stands out because it: Fully funds participation Focuses on real-world impact Supports underserved communities Provides practical entrepreneurial tools Connects you to a global ecosystem It is not just a learning program—it is a platform to scale meaningful change. Contact Information For inquiries or further details: Contact Person: Sandra Mancia Email: Sandra@Watson.is Click HERE to learn more about the Western Union Global Fellowship 2026. Conclusion The Western Union Global Fellowship 2026 is a powerful opportunity for entrepreneurs dedicated to solving some of the world’s most pressing challenges. By combining mentorship, funding support, training, and global exposure, the program empowers fellows to build sustainable solutions that uplift marginalized and displaced communities. If you are ready to scale your impact and join a global network of changemakers, this fellowship is designed for you. Discover more global Fellowship , Entrepreneurship and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY. title: Postdoctoral Research Fellow in Neurodiversity and Contextual Safeguarding at Durham University: 2-Year Research Opportunity in Applied Psychology
summary: The Postdoctoral Research Fellow in Neurodiversity and Contextual Safeguarding at Durham University is a two-year research opportunity designed for scholars seeking to advance applied, interdisciplinary research at the intersection of neurodiversity and safeguarding. Starting in September 2026, this role offers the chance to contribute to cutting-edge research that informs real-world policy and practice in youth welfare, education, and developmental psychology. About the Role This postdoctoral position is jointly hosted by the Global Centre for Contextual Safeguarding and the Centre for Neurodiversity Development within the Department of Psychology at Durham University. The role sits within an interdisciplinary research environment that brings together developmental science, psychology, and safeguarding systems reform. The position focuses on integrating neurodiversity research with contextual safeguarding approaches, which examine how young people experience safety and harm in environments beyond the home, such as schools, peer groups, and community spaces. The postholder will contribute to developing new research frameworks that explore how neurodivergent young people experience these contexts differently and how safeguarding systems can better respond. Researchers will have access to a highly supportive academic environment, including expertise in mixed methods research, participatory approaches, and advanced psychological research facilities. Why This Opportunity Matters This fellowship addresses an emerging and underexplored intersection between neurodiversity and safeguarding systems. While neurodivergent young people often face distinct vulnerabilities in educational and social settings, safeguarding frameworks have not always fully accounted for these differences. By combining contextual safeguarding with developmental psychology, this role contributes to reshaping how institutions understand safety, inclusion, and risk for neurodivergent youth. The research has direct relevance for schools, policy makers, and practitioners working to improve outcomes for young people in real-world settings. The position also reflects a broader shift in academic research toward interdisciplinary, applied work that connects theory with practical impact. It provides an opportunity to influence both academic discourse and frontline safeguarding practice. What Researchers Will Gain The postdoctoral fellow will gain experience working within a leading research environment at Durham University, one of the UK’s most respected academic institutions. The role offers the opportunity to develop an independent research profile while contributing to collaborative, high-impact projects. Researchers will engage in publishing high-quality academic outputs, developing research funding proposals, and working closely with interdisciplinary teams across psychology and sociology. The position also provides opportunities to contribute to teaching, training, and knowledge exchange activities. In addition to academic development, the role supports engagement with practitioners, policy makers, and community partners, enabling research findings to be translated into practical safeguarding approaches. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to candidates with a strong background in psychology or a related discipline. Applicants should hold a PhD or be close to completion at the time of appointment. Equivalent professional research experience may also be considered. The ideal candidate will have experience in mixed methods research, including both qualitative and quantitative approaches. Experience working with neurodivergent individuals in research contexts is highly desirable, as is familiarity with contextual safeguarding, youth development, or related fields. Strong communication skills, a commitment to inclusive research practice, and the ability to work collaboratively across disciplines are essential for this role. Eligible Areas This postdoctoral fellowship is based in the United Kingdom and requires the successful candidate to be able to work at Durham University , including in-person attendance at least two days per week. The role is open to applicants from any country, provided they meet UK eligibility requirements for employment and can contribute to research in the areas of neurodiversity, developmental psychology, and contextual safeguarding. Application Process and Deadline Applications must include a CV and a cover letter of up to two pages outlining relevant experience and alignment with the role’s requirements. Candidates must demonstrate expertise in research methods, engagement with neurodiversity or safeguarding themes, and the ability to contribute to interdisciplinary academic work. Shortlisted applicants will be invited to participate in interviews and presentations, expected to take place in early June 2026. The successful candidate will ideally begin the role on 1 September 2026. The deadline for applications is 21 May 2026 . Learn more and apply on the official Durham University job portal. title: CIP Research Fellowship 2026: $9,000 Fully Funded Remote AI Governance Research Fellowship for International Scholars
summary: The Collective Intelligence Project (CIP) has officially opened applications for its CIP Research Fellowship 2026 Pilot Cohort , offering an exciting opportunity for researchers interested in AI, public values, democratic governance, and policy innovation . This fully remote, six-month fellowship is designed for scholars who want to conduct original, publishable research using exclusive datasets that are unavailable elsewhere. Selected fellows will receive a $9,000 stipend , mentorship, co-authorship opportunities, and access to one-of-a-kind data on global public attitudes toward AI. If you are a late-stage PhD candidate, postdoctoral researcher, or independent scholar with strong research experience, this fellowship could be a major career opportunity. About the CIP Research Fellowship The Collective Intelligence Project (CIP) is seeking three research fellows for its pilot cohort. Fellows will work with proprietary datasets from CIP’s major research initiatives and produce both academic and public-facing outputs focused on how societies understand, deliberate, and govern artificial intelligence. This fellowship supports interdisciplinary work at the intersection of: Artificial Intelligence (AI) Democratic Governance Public Values Comparative Politics Computational Social Science Deliberative Research AI Policy and Regulation The program is remote and non-residential, making it accessible to researchers from around the world. Fellowship Overview Fellowship Size 3 Fellows (Pilot Cohort) Duration 6 Months Time Commitment 5–10 hours per week Bi-weekly 1-hour virtual lab meetings Format Remote Non-residential Compensation Fixed stipend of $9,000 Paid in three milestone-based tranches Application Deadline May 15, 2026 Exclusive Data Available to Fellows Selected fellows will gain internal access to three major CIP research projects: 1. Global Dialogues A recurring multi-country survey tracking public attitudes toward AI across 70+ countries with more than 7 completed rounds . Data includes: Binary votes Pairwise comparisons Open-ended responses Demographic segmentation Much of this dataset is also partially available on GitHub. 2. Collective Generation CIP’s deliberative methodology for building AI evaluation frameworks through consensus-building and public participation. Fellows will access: Deliberative session datasets Bridging consensus frameworks Public reasoning data 3. Weval An open AI evaluation platform featuring: 100+ community-contributed blueprints Testing across 112+ model configurations Performance scores range from approximately 23% to 95% , revealing major differences in how AI systems handle socially complex tasks. Fellows will receive access to: Codebase Evaluation platform Internal tools for creating and running evaluations Research Themes for Fellows Each fellow will work within a distinct thematic lens to ensure complementary research. 1. Democratic Comparative Focus areas may include: Cross-national AI governance attitudes Polarization and consensus patterns Cultural and demographic drivers of AI values Comparative deliberation outcomes 2. Communication Deliberation Potential topics include: Discourse analysis of AI values Framing patterns in public reasoning Computational text analysis across languages Perspective shifts during deliberative processes 3. Governance Institutions Research could explore: Public trust in institutions AI regulatory preferences Policy consensus on governance frameworks National and political clustering of governance preferences These themes are flexible, and strong proposals aligned with CIP’s mission are encouraged. Deliverables and Fellowship Timeline Compensation is tied to three milestones: Phase I: Onboarding (Month 1) Signed agreement Finalized 2-page Research Plan Phase II: Analysis (Months 2–4) Preliminary findings presentation to the CIP team Phase III: Final Draft (Months 5–6) A final deliverable such as: Published research paper Public blog series Working prototype Policy-oriented report Who Should Apply? CIP is primarily looking for: Late-stage PhD candidates ( ABD ) Early-career postdoctoral researchers Preferred fields include: Political Science Computational Social Science Sociology Computer Science (ML/AI) Researchers from adjacent disciplines with strong quantitative or mixed-methods expertise are also encouraged to apply. Importantly, CIP is not selecting based on institutional prestige. They are seeking researchers who can produce rigorous, original, and impactful work . International applicants are welcome. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will receive: Exclusive access to unique AI governance datasets $9,000 fixed stipend Co-authorship opportunities on CIP Working Papers Public visibility through CIP’s platforms Affiliation with a recognized AI governance organization A peer network with the other fellows Direct influence on future CIP research and data collection Required Application Materials Applicants must submit: 1. One-Page Research Proposal Include: Proposed research topic Specific dataset(s) you will use Why the research matters Novelty and impact of the project 2. CV / Resume / LinkedIn Profile Maximum: 2 pages (for CV/resume) 3. Brief Statement (200–300 words) Describe: Your experience working with large datasets Your interest in CIP’s mission and work 4. Writing Sample Examples: Academic paper Working paper Research article Analytical blog post This should demonstrate strong analytical rigor. Important Before Applying Applicants are strongly encouraged to first explore the publicly available Global Dialogues dataset on GitHub before submitting. This helps ensure your proposal is grounded in the available research infrastructure. Selection Process Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. CIP aims to contact shortlisted candidates within two weeks after the May 15 deadline . However, they reserve the right to select earlier applicants before the final deadline, so early application is strongly recommended. The CIP Research Fellowship 2026 is an outstanding opportunity for researchers passionate about shaping the future of AI governance through evidence-based scholarship. With strong financial support, exclusive datasets, global visibility, and policy relevance, this fellowship offers far more than funding—it provides a platform to influence how AI is governed worldwide. If your work sits at the intersection of AI and democracy, this is a fellowship worth applying for. Apply Now Interested candidates should prepare their proposal and submit their application before May 15, 2026 . Discover similar opportunities Here. title: ECOSOC Consultative Status 2026: Gain Access to UN Decision-Making Platforms
summary: The ECOSOC Consultative Status 2026 offers non-governmental organizations a unique opportunity to engage directly with global decision-making processes at the United Nations . Designed for NGOs seeking to influence international policy and collaborate at the highest level, this status enables organizations to contribute meaningfully to discussions shaping global development, human rights, and sustainability. About the Program The Economic and Social Council (ECOSOC) is one of the six principal organs of the United Nations, responsible for coordinating work on economic, social, and environmental issues. Through consultative status, ECOSOC creates a formal channel for NGOs to participate in its activities and those of its subsidiary bodies. This relationship is governed by ECOSOC Resolution 1996/31, which sets out the rights, responsibilities, and eligibility requirements for NGOs. Organizations granted consultative status can attend official UN meetings, submit written and oral statements, organize side events, and engage with Member States and other stakeholders. Applications submitted by 1 June 2026 will be reviewed by the ECOSOC Committee on NGOs in 2027, with final decisions made by ECOSOC. While the process can take time, it opens the door to long-term engagement with the UN system. Why This Opportunity Matters For NGOs working to create impact at local, national, or international levels, gaining ECOSOC consultative status represents a significant step toward global visibility and influence. It provides a formal channel to bring grassroots perspectives into international policy discussions and ensures that civil society voices are represented in shaping global agendas. At a time when global challenges require collaborative and inclusive solutions, this opportunity strengthens the role of NGOs in bridging the gap between communities and international institutions. What Participants Will Gain Organizations granted consultative status benefit from direct access to United Nations platforms and processes. This includes the ability to participate in key meetings such as the High-level Political Forum on Sustainable Development and various ECOSOC commissions. Beyond access, NGOs gain credibility and recognition as official contributors to global discussions. This can enhance partnerships, improve funding opportunities, and strengthen advocacy efforts. The ability to submit statements and organize side events also allows organizations to highlight their work and influence policy conversations in meaningful ways. In addition, consultative status facilitates networking with government representatives, international agencies, and other civil society actors, creating opportunities for collaboration and knowledge exchange. Who Can Apply This opportunity is open to non-governmental, non-profit organizations at the international, regional, sub-regional, and national levels. Organizations must demonstrate that their work aligns with the mission and priorities of the United Nations. Applicants should have a clear organizational structure, the ability to represent their members, and transparent governance systems. They must also be independent from government control and operate on a non-profit basis. Eligible Areas Organizations from all countries are eligible to apply, provided they meet the requirements outlined by ECOSOC. This makes the opportunity globally accessible to NGOs working across diverse sectors and regions. Application Process and Deadline To apply, organizations must complete the official online application form, ensuring that all required information and supporting documents are submitted. The application includes detailed questions about the organization’s structure, activities, and alignment with UN priorities. After submission, applications undergo an internal review process, followed by evaluation by the ECOSOC Committee on NGOs. Final decisions are made by ECOSOC based on the Committee’s recommendations. The deadline to apply is 1 June 2026 , and late submissions will not be considered for the 2027 review cycle. Given the complexity of the process, applicants are strongly encouraged to begin preparations early. Learn more on the official ECOSOC website . Explore More Opportunities for Youth Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities. The post ECOSOC Consultative Status 2026: Gain Access to UN Decision-Making Platforms appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply for the GrowthAfrica ESO Uplift Programme 2026: Free Capacity Building for ESOs in Africa
summary: Entrepreneur Support Organisations (ESOs) across Africa now have an exciting opportunity to strengthen their impact through the ESO Uplift Programme 2026 , an action-oriented capacity-building initiative by GrowthAfrica . Designed to support incubators, accelerators, innovation hubs, and business support programmes, this fully funded programme helps organisations improve their ability to support and scale social impact enterprises (SIEs) across Africa. If your organisation works with startups tackling social, environmental, or economic challenges, this programme could be the perfect opportunity to grow your impact. About GrowthAfrica Founded in 2002, GrowthAfrica is one of Africa’s leading Pan-African accelerator and entrepreneur support organisations. The organisation focuses on helping impactful enterprises grow through: Tailored business acceleration programmes Strategic business guidance Access to investment opportunities Market entry support for international companies Building strong entrepreneur support ecosystems across Africa GrowthAfrica also works closely with peer ESOs to strengthen the wider entrepreneurial ecosystem across the continent. About the ESO Uplift Programme The ESO Uplift Programme is part of the Social and Impact Entrepreneurship (SIE) Project , co-funded by the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC) . The programme is implemented by GrowthAfrica in collaboration with: Draper Richards Kaplan Foundation (DRK) Amahoro Coalition The initiative targets ESOs in: Kenya Uganda Rwanda Zambia Ghana Egypt Its main goal is to strengthen these organisations so they can better support and scale social impact enterprises. Unlike traditional training programmes, the ESO Uplift Programme follows a learning-by-doing approach , where participants apply frameworks directly within their organisations. This means participants will: Carry out practical analyses Develop implementation plans Redesign support programmes Collaborate with peer ESOs across Africa Receive mentorship and advisory support What You Will Gain The programme is designed to help build sustainable, high-performing ESOs. 1. Clarity on Your Value Creation Participants will identify: the right target group ideal geography best programme offerings This helps organisations focus their energy where it matters most. 2. Stronger Internal Capabilities Build stronger operations and improve team capacity to deliver consistent results and scale confidently. 3. Resources for Sustainability Receive practical guidance on: fundraising partnerships revenue generation impact measurement 4. Peer Learning Across Africa Join a cohort of ESOs across six countries and benefit from shared learning, collaboration, and cross-border insights. 5. Continued Support After the Programme At the end of the programme, 5 ESOs will be selected to continue receiving dedicated support from GrowthAfrica. 6. No Participation Fee Thanks to co-funding by the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation, participation is completely free of charge . Programme Impact at a Glance GrowthAfrica has already made significant impact across Africa: 27 team members 7 countries 100+ projects delivered 5,000+ entrepreneurs supported 45,000+ jobs created Programme Structure The programme runs for 12 months , beginning in July 2026 , and is divided into three practical phases. Phase 1: Initial Analysis Time Commitment: Approximately 4 hours per week Includes: 4 virtual workshops (2.5 hours each) Individual follow-up and mentoring sessions Pre-reading and practical materials Identification of gaps in your current offerings This phase helps organisations understand where improvements are needed. Phase 2: Programme Redesign Time Commitment: Approximately 2–3 hours per week Includes: Planning programme redesign with your team Individual advisory sessions Peer experience-sharing sessions This stage focuses on redesigning your existing support programmes for stronger impact. Phase 3: Programme Execution Time Commitment: Flexible Includes: Submission of implementation plans for feedback Launch of your revised programme Monthly check-ins for ongoing support This final stage ensures real implementation and sustainable change. Eligibility Criteria The programme is open to organisations running: incubators accelerators innovation hubs business support programmes You do not need to identify specifically as an ESO—what matters is the work you do. To qualify, your organisation must meet the following criteria: 1. Registered and Operating Your organisation must be legally registered and actively operating in: Kenya Uganda Rwanda Zambia Ghana Egypt 2. You Support Social Impact Enterprises You must work with businesses that generate revenue while intentionally addressing: social challenges economic challenges environmental challenges 3. At Least 2 Years of Experience Your organisation should have a minimum of two years of experience supporting startups or post-revenue ventures through: acceleration mentorship training 4. Senior Leadership Commitment A CEO, Co-founder, or Managing Director must attend all workshops to ensure strategic continuity and decision-making authority. 5. Working Proficiency in English The entire programme is conducted in English, so participants must have working proficiency. How to Apply Interested organisations can APPLY HERE Early applications are strongly encouraged, especially for organisations looking to strengthen their support systems and scale their impact across Africa. The GrowthAfrica ESO Uplift Programme 2026 is more than just a training programme—it is a strategic opportunity for Entrepreneur Support Organisations to redesign, strengthen, and future-proof their support systems. With no participation fee, expert mentorship, peer learning, and the potential for continued post-programme support, this initiative offers exceptional value for ESOs committed to building stronger social impact ecosystems across Africa. If your organisation is ready to level up its impact, now is the time to apply. Deadline: 31st May 2026 Discover similar opportunities Here The post Apply for the GrowthAfrica ESO Uplift Programme 2026: Free Capacity Building for ESOs in Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
